Currently we have made 20150lbs if CAR.  We need a additional 11850 lbs to finish the order Justin and Katya

Items/Learnings Discussed with THG Tech (Cornell): 
1. Standardize maintenance checking folding clips before the start of each run/start of each shift.

 (Photo 1A)

2. Ridged grippers should be cleaned especially if the surface has become smooth and product is being unseated/launched as a result.



(Photos 2A & 2B)

3. Caramels with jagged edges or uneven sides, can also be unseated at the grabber head.

(Photo 3A)

4. Clamp for wrapping arms should be cleared of any build-up; especially if we’re seeing loose single piece wraps flying out. This is an indication they're not entering into the clamp properly.



(Photos 4A & 4B)

5. Bequet wrap only features a thin line as a gap between each individual piece, clean the sensor where the wrap feeds; if you're seeing issues with the wrap.

(Photo 5A) 

6. If green ends are not at the ends of the caramel piece; turn roller clock-wise if the green wrap is on the west side, and turn roller counter clock-wise if the green wrap is bunching on east side.








(Photos 6A, 6B, 6C & 6D)

7. Speed for THG belts to be adjusted in accordance with the rate given per Supply speed not line speed alone. For example, while running today our line speed was 0.9, however, the speed being registered— by the sensor between Tunnel 2 & THG belts—never exceeded 820, so the tech had our speed set between 700-750. 




(Photos 7A & 7B)
Viscosity, WA, Brix

This was happening last week, too, right? Do you remember what the findings were?

When one of the tracking sensors is triggered for too long it will fault, if both tracking sensors are triggered it faults after a certain time. Basically we have to adjust the tracking so that it favors one side or the other and gets forced back and forth with the tracking actuation.

Cute Cavanna is now running. The Truffle Line lead explained that the flow wrap was installed incorrectly. The rollers indicate the order in which it should be loaded, but the current markings are not accurate. I will ask her to remove them and relabel them in the correct order.
She also adjusted the roll position. There are currently several marks showing where the roll should be placed, but the area will be cleaned and only one reference mark will be left to make it easier to identify the correct position.
It is also important that the film runs on both sides of the guide plate between the rails. The first shift lead went over all of this with the third shift lead and showed her the correct setup.
